latest.title: Result of AGM
latest.content:
11 Dec 2025 17:15
RNS Number : 2737L
Alternative Liquidity Fund Limited
11 December 2025
ALTERNATIVE LIQUIDITY FUND LIMITED
Registered in Guernsey - Number 60552
LEI NUMBER 213800R5CHD76J3LU713
Registered Office:
1 Royal Plaza, Royal Avenue,
St Peter Port, Guernsey, GY1 2HL
11 December 2025
ANNUAL GENERAL MEETING VOTING RESULTS
The Board of Alternative Liquidity Fund Limited (the "
Company
") is pleased to announce the results of the Annual General Meeting ("
AGM
") which was held at the registered office of the Company at 11.00am on 11 December 2025. Resolutions 1 to 4 were proposed as Ordinary Resolutions and Resolution 5 was proposed as a Special Resolution.
The result of the voting was:
Resolution 1: THAT the Directors be and are hereby authorised to re-appoint Grant Thornton as the Company's auditor, and to fix the auditors' remuneration
Votes for: 85,167,374 (92.34%)
Votes against: 7,060,247
Votes withheld: NIL
Total Votes Cast: 92,227,621
Result: resolution passed
Resolution 2: THAT Mr Quentin Spicer be re-elected as a Director of the Company
Votes for: 85,158,362 (92.33%)
Votes against: 7,069,259
Votes withheld: NIL
Total Votes Cast: 92,227,621
Result: resolution passed
Resolution 3: THAT Dr Richard Berman be re-elected as a Director of the Company
Votes for: 85,167,374 (92.34%)
Votes against: 7,060,247
Votes withheld: NIL
Total Votes Cast: 92,227,621
Result: resolution passed
Resolution 4: THAT Mr Anthony Pickford be re-elected as a Director of the Company
Votes for: 85,167,374 (92.34%)
Votes against: 7,060,247
Votes withheld: NIL
Total Votes Cast: 92,227,621
Result: resolution passed
Resolution 5: THAT the Company is approved to continue in existence and the continued implementation by the Board of the Investment Policy and Strategy be approved.
Votes for: 92,227,621 (100.00%)
Votes against: NIL
Votes withheld: NIL
Total Votes Cast: 92,227,621
Result: resolution passed

Fund Name
NAV
Quarterly Performance
Alternative Liquidity Fund Limited
USD 0.1569

As previously announced, realisation of assets within the Company's portfolio since the end of June, together with the announcement of a distribution to shareholders of $18.99mln being equivalent to $0.13 (13 cents) a share, result in the June 30 NAV being significantly different to the current position. Therefore, in addition to publishing the June 30 NAV, the Board has taken the decision to provide an indicative NAV as at September 30 that incorporates changes to the underlying portlio over the past quarter.
The Board estimates that as at the close of business on 30 September 2025, the indicative Net Asset Value per share of the Company is as follows:
Fund Name
NAV
Quarterly Performance
Alternative Liquidity Fund Limited
USD 0.0365
154% - see Note
Note: the indicative quarterly performance for Sep 30 comprises the distribution to shareholders plus a slight uplift in the underlying portfolio value.
An analysis of the portfolio as at June 30 and September 30 2025 is provided in the quarterly factsheet which is now available on the Company's website, please see the link below.
https://alternativeliquidityfund.com/fact-sheets-and-fund-updates/2025/
Please note that the fact sheet will contain the detailed portfolio update, the Board's observations and conclusions as indicated in the previous shareholder notification of September 22nd.
The final September NAV will be published in due course.

latest.title: Receipt of Funds and Return of Capital
latest.content:
22 Sep 2025 17:20
RNS Number : 3167A
Alternative Liquidity Fund Limited
22 September 2025
ALTERNATIVE LIQUIDITY FUND LIMITED
Registered in Guernsey - Number 60552
LEI NUMBER 213800R5CHD76J3LU713
Registered Office:
1 Royal Plaza, Royal Avenue,
St Peter Port, Guernsey, GY1 2HL
22 September 2025
RECEIPT OF FUNDS AND RETURN OF CAPITAL
The Board of Directors of Alternative Liquidity Fund Limited is pleased to announce that the Company has received a distribution of US$2.7 million from the V Invest RJ Fund and US$18.59 million from Vision FCVS RJ Fund.
It has been resolved to return an amount of US$13 cents per share ($0.13) to shareholders, being US$18.99 million in total, based on the current number of shares in issue. This return of capital will be effected by way of an issue of redeemable B shares to existing shareholders
pro-rata
to their shareholding on the record date set out below and the subsequent redemption and cancellation of those B Shares.
At the next board meeting (8 October 2025) the Board and the Investment Advisor will discuss the remaining holdings in the portfolio, the potential outcomes and the estimated timing of their monetization. The Investment Advisor will then prepare a detailed shareholder report which will be released via the Exchange, detailing the discussion and the Board's recommendation to shareholders with regards to the Company's future.
It is expected that the timetable for the return of capital will be as follows:
25 September 2025
Ex date
26 September 2025
Record date for shareholders on the register to receive B shares
29 September 2025
B shares allotted and issued to shareholders on the register as at the Record date
30 September 2025
B shares redeemed and cancelled
31 October 2025
Redemption payment made
All redemption payments will be paid gross of tax.
Enquiries
Tim Gardner, Hindsight Solutions Limited
Tel. +44 7917 630 460
Email:
timgardner@hindsightsolutions.co.uk
Website:
​
https://alternativeliquidityfund.com/
Apex Fund and Corporate Services (Guernsey) Limited
Telephone: +44 (0) 20 3530 3600

company_30s.what_they_do: Allanfield Group is an AIM‑listed investment vehicle that builds and manages a diversified portfolio of small‑cap UK companies.  Its main job is to acquire stakes in emerging businesses, provide them with capital and management support, and then return value to shareholders through dividends or share buy‑backs.
company_30s.why_they_matter: Small‑cap companies often grow faster than larger peers but are harder to access for ordinary investors.  Allanfield offers a professionally managed channel to that segment, giving investors a single point of entry into a basket of high‑growth assets that would otherwise be difficult to find or evaluate.
company_30s.current_state: The company has been actively managing its capital base, returning cash to shareholders and tightening its holdings.  Shares trade at a modest fraction of the fund’s net asset value, suggesting the market has priced in a significant discount.  The latest updates point to a focus on cash generation and portfolio consolidation.

rns_narrative: The AGM and subsequent updates reveal a consistent theme of capital management – returning cash to shareholders, tightening the portfolio, and keeping the NAV stable.
The company is also keeping investors informed on its holdings and portfolio changes, showing a transparent approach to asset selection.
Recent filings indicate no new acquisitions or major debt issuances, suggesting a pause in aggressive expansion.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Silence – the community is quiet, with no active chatter or hype.
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: Only a handful of old posts from the IPO era praise the discount to NAV, but there is no current bullish dialogue.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: The absence of recent negative commentary means no fresh bear case is circulating, though the small‑cap nature still raises liquidity concerns.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: The sentiment has remained flat – no noticeable shift toward optimism or pessimism.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends shows no search interest, indicating a dead market and a high chance of surprise if a catalyst hits.

near_term_catalysts: The AGM results on 11 Dec 2025 could confirm a capital return or a dividend declaration, giving the stock a lift.
The NAV factsheet on 19 Dec 2025 will reveal whether the discount to value is narrowing.
Any announcement of a new investment or a divestment that improves the portfolio mix could act as a trigger.
A sustained spike in trading volume (e.g., 2‑3× the 20‑day average) would signal renewed interest.
expect_next.watch_for: Key signals to monitor over the next 2–3 months: a breakout above the 0.07 p level, a volume surge, or a positive change in the NAV that reduces the discount.
expect_next.timing_regime: The stock is still in the early phase of a deep crash; the APEX score shows low compression, meaning the mean reversion may still be a year away.  A clear catalyst is needed to start the recovery.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: The shares have fallen more than 70 % from their peak, a classic deep‑crash scenario that historically delivers 4‑6× returns once the market reverts to fundamentals.  The company’s recent cash‑return strategy signals confidence, and the dead market offers a surprise element.  If the stock recovers to 50 % of its pre‑crash level, the upside is roughly 5× the current price.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: The remaining downside is limited to the current price if the stock stays in the same pocket, but liquidity risk and the possibility of further dilution remain.  A prolonged period of flat trading could keep the discount entrenched.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential upside: ~5×.  Potential downside: ~80 % of current price (the amount already lost).  The asymmetry is roughly 1 : 5, favouring the long side.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter when the price clears 0.07 p on volume that is at least 2× the 20‑day average.  Size the position at 5 % of your discretionary equity and place a stop 0.05 p to protect against a brief dip.
playbook.if_watching: Hold until the AGM on 11 Dec 2025.  If a capital return or dividend is announced, consider a partial entry at 0.06 p with a tight stop.
playbook.if_passing: None – there are no imminent delisting or bankruptcy signals.

ai_insights.bottom_line: A deep‑crash, silent‑market setup with a capital‑return catalyst offers a 5× upside versus an 80 % downside, making it a contrarian bullish play with a 1 : 5 risk/reward.
